name: EASY PEASY PASTA
description: The clue is in the name with this one! It's an effortlessly simple pasta sauce that can be made from most of BOSH HQ's mostly pot-roasted vegetables. It's a regular supper at BOSH HQ. It's fresh, filling and gives you loads of your daily vegetables in one lavish meal. Try serving it up with a side salad and some crusty bread.
ingredients: 400g wholemeal pasta (we like sun-dried tomato fusilli)
method: Preheat oven to 180°C | 20 x 30cm lasagne dish | Large saucepan

Peel and finely slice the onions and garlic. Cut the peppers in half, cut out the stems and seeds and slice into thin strips. Slice the sun-dried tomatoes. Finely dice the courgettes. Roughly chop the baby leaf spinach and basil and put them into the colander ready to wash them.

Put all the vegetables into the lasagne dish and season all over with salt and pepper. Pour over the oil and toss everything in it to make sure the vegetables are covered. Cover the dish with foil and put it in the oven to roast for 30 minutes.
Take the dish out of the oven, remove the foil, stir everything and put the dish back in the oven for a further 15 minutes.

Meanwhile, boil the kettle and pour the boiling water into a large pan on a high heat. Add the pasta and a big pinch of salt and cook until al dente, following the instructions on the packet. Drain the cooked pasta through a colander and tip it back into the pan.
Take the lasagne dish out of the oven, stir in the basil leaves and pour your freshly roasted veggie sauce over the pasta. Stir so that it's well mixed. Serve and enjoy!
